_comment: World B usage-aware cascade. Routing policy lives in routing-policy.json; this file supplies the executable cascade classes. A chunk's executor intent maps to a class here; cascade-dispatch.sh walks that class's role ladder, resolves roles per host via harness-profile.json, checks live headroom (usage-probe.sh) + the quality_floor, dispatches the best available rung, and on a cap error fires the Airlift Tier-1 checkpoint and DESCENDS. OpenRouter mechanical-agent routing in dm-review reads the same policy; DeepSeek names are model slugs, never a separate provider.

class_from_kind._comment: Coding kind -> cascade class. Config/docs go OpenRouter-primary; logic, ui, and integration go Codex-primary. Claude is non-coding-only and appears in no executable coding class.

quality_rank._comment: Global model->score; enforces quality_floor regardless of which role surfaced the model. Higher = better. Bare slugs (gpt-5.6-sol) are the Codex-native names; openai/-prefixed slugs are the same models via the OpenRouter wrapper -- both carry the same score.
quality_rank._comment_gpt56: GPT-5.6 (Sol/Terra/Luna, GA 2026-07-09) supersedes GPT-5.5. Sol is the quality head at $5/$30 per 1M and 1.05M context. GPT-5.5 is retained as the older-codex-cli native fallback, but on the paid API ladder Terra precedes it: both score 55 on Artificial Analysis v4.1 at max effort, while Terra costs half as much ($2.50/$15 versus $5/$30). Luna ($1/$6) and GLM-5.2 (about $0.91/$2.86 live on OpenRouter 2026-07-18) both score 51; price breaks the quality tie in GLM's favour, so Luna remains at the frontier_api tail rather than cheap_api.
quality_rank._comment_codex_mini: gpt-5.1-codex-mini keeps a rank so a stray re-add scores above the codex floor rather than silently resolving to 0, but it appears in NO ladder: `codex exec --model gpt-5.1-codex-mini` returns HTTP 400 'not supported when using Codex with a ChatGPT account', and the codex host runs on the ChatGPT sub. Do not re-add it to harness-profile.json.
quality_rank._comment_kimi_k3: Kimi K3 (moonshotai/kimi-k3, Moonshot AI, GA 2026-07-16) is the planned-open-weight, API-only-today quality head for OpenRouter: 2.8T-param MoE, native vision, 1M context, $3/$15 with $0.30 cache hits, and max-only reasoning. Artificial Analysis v4.1 scores K3 at 57: behind Fable 5 (60) and GPT-5.6 Sol max (59), ahead of Opus 4.8 max (56), Terra/GPT-5.5 max (55), Sonnet 5 max (53), and GLM-5.2 max (51). Rank 97 reflects quality-first placement; price breaks ties, not cross-tier quality gaps. K3 leads openrouter_exec and sits second on frontier_api, while GLM remains the cheaper default for mechanical review and bulk work. OpenRouter currently shows one K3 provider and warns of limited upstream capacity, so every K3 placement has a fallback. The current wrapper is text-only despite model multimodality. PRIVACY: provider privacy is demoted; OPENROUTER_ZDR=1 remains opt-in.

codex._comment: Primary executor = Codex for logic, UI, and integration. When Codex caps, descend through agentic and wrapper OpenRouter models; Claude has no coding rung.

openrouter._comment: Primary executor = OpenRouter for config/docs/mechanical logic. Codex is the trusted coding fallback; Claude has no coding rung.

policy._comment_headroom: Treat a sub/native rail as exhausted once probed remaining quota drops below this -- hand off BEFORE the hard wall (mirrors Airlift's early-warning trip).
policy._comment_reentry: When an exhausted sub rail's resets_at passes it re-enters at its original ladder position (flat-rate and free again -- prefer quality).
policy._comment_subscriptions: Coding rails: Codex Pro 20x is the active flat-rate workhorse, with a named 5x profile; OpenRouter carries external agentic and mechanical work. Claude Pro is reserved for non-coding tasks and is absent from coding cascades.
policy._comment_fable: Fable 5 keeps its quality score for research and non-coding comparison only. Claude is outside the executable coding graph, so availability never adds Fable or Opus to a coding ladder.
policy._comment_native_host_constraint: GPT-5.6 Sol leads Codex-native and OpenRouter paid coding ladders. Claude aliases retained in harness-profile native_judgment are compatibility metadata for explicit non-coding workflows and are referenced by no coding cascade.
policy.privacy: DEMOTED (2026-07-18, user directive): model selection priority is Quality > Price > Speed > Provider privacy. OPENROUTER_ZDR is OPT-IN, never a default pin -- Chinese first-party hosting (Moonshot/DeepSeek/Z.AI) is acceptable by default. Set OPENROUTER_ZDR=1 per-call only for genuinely sensitive material (client code under NDA, credentials-adjacent diffs); when set, a model with no eligible ZDR provider (e.g. moonshotai/kimi-k3 pre-weights) is a per-model failure and the ladder walks on.
policy._comment_headroom_unknown: usage-probe state 'unknown' is treated as HAS-headroom: dispatch plus reactive cap detection is preferred over falsely skipping a healthy Codex or OpenRouter rail.
policy._comment_agentic_seam: openrouter_exec is the agentic OpenRouter rung for config/docs/mechanical-logic. Wrapper rungs (cheap_api/frontier_api) remain single-turn analysis/text generation and are still invalid for complex logic/ui/integration implementation.
